PARTY CONDITION: Full HP, all spell slots and class features restored after long rest on arrival. No active conditions, no exhaustion. Morale stable — two shardseas cleared, pattern understood, but the leyline corruption vision raises stakes. Party enters Shard C at peak readiness with open logistical needs and no local intelligence yet.

CAMPAIGN CONTEXT: The Shardsea of Governance is power in institutional form — trade routes, naval authority, and shard-politics competing across a maritime zone that functions as civilization's nervous system. Unlike Elements or Nature's Wrath, the danger here is not primal or corrupted; it is human, bureaucratic, and layered with competing agendas. Merchants, naval commanders, and political figures all have stakes in who controls these waters. The party arrives with no local contacts and no specific mission brief — first contact with Shard C's power structure will define what the threat is and who, if anyone, is an ally. The Compass of Lost Tides' leyline vision haunts the background: two shards of corruption cleared, the purple miasma still spreading, and Governance as the third trial implies that whatever is corrupting the world's foundations may have institutional as well as elemental dimensions.
